title: Thermophotovoltaic efficiency of 40%
authors_or_org: LaPotin, A.; Schulte, K.L.; Steiner, M.A.; Buznitsky, K.; Kelsall, C.C.; Friedman, D.J.; Tervo, E.J.; France, R.M.; Young, M.R.; Rohskopf, A.; Verma, S.; Wang, E.N.; Henry, A.
claim_supported: 1.4/1.2 eV tandem TPV cell reached 41.1+/-1% efficiency at 2.39 W/cm^2 power density and 2400C emitter temperature; 1.2/1.0 eV device reached 39.3+/-1% at 2127C -- record TPV efficiencies exceeding typical steam-turbine efficiency.
locator: Abstract and results tables, accessed via PMC mirror PMC9007744
notes: Nature.com required login redirect; full text confirmed via open-access PMC mirror. Foundational TPV efficiency record cited by essentially all subsequent thermal-battery/TPV demand-side coverage (Antora, Fourth Power).

title: High-efficiency air-bridge thermophotovoltaic cells
authors_or_org: Roy-Layinde, B.; Lim, J.; Arneson, C.; Forrest, S.R.; Lenert, A. (University of Michigan)
claim_supported: Air-bridge TPV cell design achieves up to 44% conversion efficiency at emitter temperatures below 1500C, a lower-temperature alternative to the LaPotin et al. 2400C tandem-cell approach; Joule, Vol 8, Issue 7, pp. 2135-2145, published 2024-05-22.
locator: Abstract/summary via search aggregation of ScienceDirect and University of Michigan press coverage
notes: Page not directly fetched this session (identified via WebSearch synthesis of Cell Press/ScienceDirect/UMich listings); DOI and venue appear consistently across three independent index pages, but verifier should confirm by direct fetch.

title: Research Advances in the Application of the Supercritical CO2 Brayton Cycle to Reactor Systems: A Review
claim_supported: Review of sCO2 Brayton cycle applications across reactor systems (SMR, Gen IV, fusion); DOI confirmed valid via doi.org redirect to MDPI Energies 16(21):7367.
locator: Article landing page, Energies vol 16 issue 21 article 7367
notes: MDPI page itself returned 403 on direct fetch; DOI resolution confirmed via WebFetch redirect-detection to the correct MDPI URL, which is independent evidence the DOI is real (not invented).

title: Flow and heat transfer performance of supercritical CO2 recuperator with serpentine channels at high thermal-hydraulic parameters
claim_supported: New printed-circuit-heat-exchanger (PCHE) serpentine-channel recuperator correlations for sCO2 recuperators, applicable to wider parameter ranges than prior Nu/friction-factor correlations.
locator: Abstract, Applied Thermal Engineering (2025), pii S1359431125034787
notes: Abstract-only page accessible without subscription; full text behind paywall.

title: Recent Progress on High Temperature and High Pressure Heat Exchangers for Supercritical CO2 Power Generation and Conversion Systems

claim_supported: Screening of candidate materials for thermally/chemically stable TPV optical emitters operating at very high temperature (bears on thermal-battery emitter durability, a key TPV bottleneck).

title: Supercritical CO2 Power Cycles (program overview)
authors_or_org: US DOE National Energy Technology Laboratory (NETL)
claim_supported: NETL sCO2 Brayton power cycle program page: describes the GTI/SwRI/GE-led pilot as the largest-scale sCO2 demonstration to date, targeting a scalable pathway to 10s-100s MW, with recompression closed Brayton cycle (RCBC) operation at 715C turbine inlet temperature and 10 MW output.

title: "We've Got the Power": Sandia technology test delivers electricity to the grid
claim_supported: On 2022-04-12, Sandia's sCO2 Brayton-cycle test loop delivered up to 10 kW to the Sandia-Kirtland grid at 600F for about 50 minutes across three controlled start/stop cycles -- a step toward 1-5 MW commercial-scale systems.
notes: Directly fetched; primary national-lab source.

title: 초임계 이산화탄소 발전 시스템, 국내 최초 '분기점' 달성 (Supercritical CO2 Power Generation System Achieves Korea's First 'Break-Even Point')
claim_supported: KAERI, with consortium partners Jinsol Turbo Machinery, KAIST, and POSTECH, achieved a 100 kW sCO2 power-generation 'break-even point' (output power exceeding input power) in April 2024, reported 2024-10-23 -- Korea's first such demonstration, compact to 1/10-1/20 the size of comparable steam systems.
locator: Press release body, kaeri.re.kr board post, menuId MENU00326 linkId 12154
notes: Directly fetched via Korean-language query; primary Korean national-lab demonstration record with named industry/academic consortium partners.

title: 工程热物理所在超临界 CO2 回热器热惯性研究方面取得进展 (Progress on Thermal Inertia of Supercritical CO2 Recuperator at the Institute of Engineering Thermophysics)
authors_or_org: Institute of Engineering Thermophysics, Chinese Academy of Sciences (CAS-IET)
claim_supported: CAS Institute of Engineering Thermophysics, with Xi'an Jiaotong University and Tsinghua University, engineered a hundred-kilowatt-class rapid-response PCHE recuperator for sCO2 Brayton cycles: up to 22C/min permissible heating rate, 129.4 kW peak duty, >95% heat-exchange efficiency, stabilizing within 30-60 seconds after sudden flow changes; work funded under CAS Youth Team Program and National Key R&D Program (No. 2022YFB4100100), published 2025-01-26.
notes: Directly fetched via Chinese-language query; primary Chinese national-lab R&D record with named university partners and grant number.

title: Performance Test Codes (PTC 6, PTC 6.2, PTC 22, PTC 6S)
claim_supported: ASME publishes 48 Performance Test Codes covering power production, combustion/heat transfer, fluid handling, and emissions, including PTC 6 (steam turbines), PTC 6.2 (steam turbines in combined cycles), and PTC 22 (gas turbines); no sCO2-specific PTC was identified in this session, indicating a possible standards gap for sCO2 turbomachinery acceptance testing.
locator: ASME Codes & Standards – Performance Test Codes landing page
notes: Directly fetched. The apparent absence of an sCO2-specific PTC is a notable gap worth flagging in the lane brief bottlenecks section, though absence-of-evidence should be treated cautiously (ASME may have newer/draft codes not indexed on this page).

title: Part 53 – Risk-Informed, Technology-Inclusive Regulatory Framework for Commercial Nuclear Plants
claim_supported: NRC finalized 10 CFR Part 53, a new risk-informed, technology-inclusive licensing framework for advanced reactors, on 2026-03-26, effective 2026-04-29 -- a regulatory trigger relevant to licensing non-LWR balance-of-plant/power-conversion systems (sCO2, molten-salt, sodium-cooled).
locator: NRC Part 53 landing page (content via WebSearch synthesis of ANS Nuclear Newswire and Federal Register listing; direct fetch to nrc.gov timed out and federalregister.gov redirected to an unblock/verification page)
notes: Not independently fetched this session; effective date and rule number should be reconfirmed by the verifier via a successful direct fetch.

claim_supported: EIA "Today in Energy" analysis on how enhanced geothermal systems (EGS), using oil-and-gas horizontal-drilling and fracking techniques, could expand US geothermal power generation beyond conventional hydrothermal sites.
locator: Article body (content via WebSearch synthesis; not independently fetched this session)
notes: Not fetched directly; verifier should confirm figures via direct fetch.

claim_supported: DOE NEPA categorical-exclusion record (dated 2024-08-28) for Antora Energy's ARPA-E SCALEUP award to scale pilot production of its combined heat-and-power thermal battery (solid-state heat engine / TPV), which converts stored carbon-block heat into on-demand power for industrial facilities.
notes: Directly fetched; exact dollar figure not shown on this specific page (Antora's own site cites $14.5M, but that page could not be independently fetched -- see L04-046 notes).

title: Firebrick Resistance-heated Energy Storage (FIRES)
claim_supported: ARPA-E FIRES program page confirms Electrified Thermal Solutions as an awardee developing electrically conductive firebrick ("E-brick") thermal-storage/resistance-heating technology; the fetched page did not disclose the specific award dollar amount.

notes: Page fetch returned only a thin excerpt (header/nav); award amount should be confirmed by the verifier via the full ARPA-E project PDF or press release.

title: Renewables for Industry – Executive Summary
claim_supported: Low-temperature-heat sectors are ~70% of global industrial energy consumption and generated ~3 Gt CO2 in 2023 (half of direct industrial emissions, an 8% decline since 2013); thermal storage costs ~$15-20/kWh vs. chemical batteries; EU heat-electrification technical potential is ~600 TWh/yr additional electricity demand, China's is ~1,700 TWh/yr (comparable to projected solar PV growth to 2030); industrial heat-pump operating costs in the EU span EUR41-74/MWh.
notes: Directly fetched; treated as source_type government since IEA is an intergovernmental agency analogous to a national statistical/policy body -- flagged for verifier to confirm classification fit. Figures are load-bearing market/technology-cost data used for triangulation against the China-specific market estimate in L04-051.

title: Energy Efficiency Grant Call for Power Generation Companies
claim_supported: Singapore's EMA runs a dedicated Energy Efficiency Grant Call specifically for power-generation companies, alongside NEA's broader Energy Efficiency Grant (co-funding up to 70% of qualifying equipment cost, capped per category) -- a concrete government-backed procurement/co-funding channel for waste-heat-to-power and efficiency retrofits in Singapore.
locator: Grant call landing page (content via WebSearch synthesis; not independently fetched this session)
notes: English is an official administrative language in Singapore, so this does not count toward the zh/ja/ko local-language quota, though it is a genuine Singapore-geography primary government source.

title: भाभा परमाणु अनुसंधान केंद्र (भा.प.अ.कें.) (BARC) की गतिविधियाँ – आर एंड डी (R&D activities of the Bhabha Atomic Research Centre)
authors_or_org: Bhabha Atomic Research Centre (BARC), Government of India
claim_supported: BARC's R&D pages describe work under India's three-stage nuclear program, including molten-salt breeder reactor (IMSBR) concepts that use a supercritical-CO2-based Brayton cycle (SCBC) for power conversion.
locator: Hindi-language R&D activities page (content via WebSearch synthesis; not independently fetched this session)
notes: Found via Hindi-language query; not one of the zh/ja/ko languages specified for the local-language quota, but included for India-geography primary-government coverage. Verifier should fetch directly to confirm SCBC-specific content location on the site.

title: Supercritical CO2 Brayton Power Cycle Pilot Plant Bolstered with $80M in Federal Funding
claim_supported: DOE/NETL awarded up to $80 million (announced 2016-10-18) to a 10 MWe sCO2 Brayton-cycle pilot plant at SwRI's San Antonio campus, led by GTI with GE Global Research, targeting >50% cycle efficiency at a minimum 700C turbine inlet temperature -- advancing the tech "from proof-of-concept to a validated prototype operational system."
notes: Directly fetched; corroborates and adds detail to the NETL program page (L04-026).

title: STEP Demo pilot plant achieves full operational conditions for Phase 1 of testing
claim_supported: On 2024-10-16, the $169M STEP Demo sCO2 pilot plant (GTI Energy, SwRI, GE Vernova Advanced Research, DOE/NETL) reached full operating conditions: ~4 MW net power synchronized to the grid, 500C turbine inlet temperature, 27,000 rpm.
notes: Directly fetched; SwRI is the operating research institute for the demonstration, making this a primary project-status record rather than pure trade press -- tiered T1.

title: NET Power's First Allam Cycle 300-MW Gas-Fired Project Will Be Built in Texas
claim_supported: NET Power's first commercial 300 MW Allam-cycle plant (Project Permian/"Serial Number One") is being built at an Occidental-hosted site near Odessa, TX; captures ~860,000 t CO2/yr; construction started Q3 2024, targeted online 2026-2028; industry capex estimates for similar Allam-cycle plants run $900-1,200/kW; financing to combine NET Power capital, shareholder investment, project financing, and potential 45Q tax credits.
notes: Directly fetched.

title: NET Power Inc. – Form 10-Q, Quarter Ended March 31, 2026
authors_or_org: NET Power Inc. (US SEC EDGAR filing)
claim_supported: NET Power Inc.'s Q1 2026 10-Q filing (audited/certified quarterly report) should contain the most current capex, milestone, and Project Permian/Odessa status disclosures for NET Power's Allam-cycle sCO2 plant.
locator: SEC EDGAR filing index (direct fetch returned HTTP 403; content not independently verified this session)
notes: SEC EDGAR blocked automated WebFetch access. Record retained as a company_filing lead; verifier must independently retrieve and confirm before any load-bearing claim is drawn from it.

title: Rondo deploys Southeast Asia's 'first industrial Heat Battery'
claim_supported: Rondo Energy deployed a 33 MWh Rondo Heat Battery at SCG Cleanergy's cement plant in Saraburi, Thailand -- described as the first commercial heat-battery system at a cement facility, integrated with existing heat-recovery infrastructure to drive a steam turbine, reported 2025-11-17.
notes: Thailand is not one of the six core Asian markets (CN/JP/KR/TW/IN/SG) named in the mission's Asia-geography assumption, so this record is not counted toward that specific 8-source quota, but it is retained as directly relevant Southeast Asian demand evidence for the heat-to-power lane.

title: Rondo Energy turns on first major thermal battery — at an oil field
claim_supported: Rondo Energy's 100 MWh Heat Battery began daily automatic solar-powered operation around October 2025, delivering high-pressure industrial steam to a fuel-production facility in California -- described as the world's largest industrial heat battery.
locator: Article body (content via general WebSearch synthesis; a dedicated WebFetch attempt on this exact URL returned HTTP 403 this session)
notes: Fetch attempt failed (403); claim is based on cross-corroborated WebSearch snippets (Canary Media and Energy-Storage.News both reported the same 100 MWh California milestone), but verifier should independently fetch to confirm exact customer name and date.

title: Fervo enters 1.75-GW geothermal turbine supply deal with Turboden
claim_supported: Fervo Energy will buy 1.75 GW of Organic Rankine Cycle turbines over three years from Turboden America (Mitsubishi Heavy Industries Group), announced 2026-04-07/09 -- expanding on an earlier order covering 150 MW of capacity for Fervo's Cape Station (Utah) project, part of a planned 500 MW enhanced-geothermal facility.
notes: Directly fetched; one of the largest disclosed heat-to-power turbine procurement commitments found this session.

title: TerraPower Announces Contract Awards for Natrium Vendors
claim_supported: TerraPower's Natrium Reactor Demonstration Project (345 MWe sodium-cooled fast reactor with molten-salt energy storage, Kemmerer, WY) awarded vendor contracts (announced 2023-08-01) including BWXT Canada for the Intermediate Heat Exchanger and Curtiss-Wright Flow Control Service for the Reactor Protection System -- direct balance-of-plant procurement evidence for an advanced-reactor power-conversion/storage project.
notes: Directly fetched; primary company press release, tiered T1 as an official project-award announcement.

title: Turboden to design and produce Organic Rankine Cycle system for Canadian oil and gas producer, Strathcona Resources Ltd.
claim_supported: Turboden will design/produce a ~19 MW single-shaft ORC waste-heat-to-power system (North America's largest single-shaft ORC turbine) for Strathcona Resources' Orion thermal-oil facility near Cold Lake, Alberta, offsetting ~80% of site grid-power consumption and an estimated ~740,000 t CO2e over the project lifetime; announced 2024-01-10, completion targeted H1 2025.
notes: Directly fetched; primary vendor/company press release, tiered T1 as an official procurement announcement. Geography tagged 'multinational' because Turboden America is a Mitsubishi Heavy Industries (Japan) subsidiary selling into a Canadian oil-and-gas customer.

title: Hanwha Power Systems, TC Energy Commercialize sCO2 Power Generation with Waste Heat Recovery
claim_supported: Hanwha Power Systems (South Korea) and TC Energy signed an MOU to develop an sCO2 waste-heat-recovery power system at a natural-gas pipeline compressor station, building on Hanwha's Turbo Expander Generator (TEG) and sCO2 Engine product lines.
locator: Article body (content via WebSearch synthesis; direct WebFetch returned HTTP 403 this session)
notes: Fetch failed (403); Hanwha is a South Korean conglomerate subsidiary, giving this record KR geography alongside the US customer (TC Energy).

title: Chaotan One: World's 1st supercritical CO2 power unit starts in China
claim_supported: China's 'Chaotan One' (超碳一号) sCO2 waste-heat power demonstration comprises two 15 MW units (30 MW total) at a steel factory in Liupanshui, Guizhou Province, developed by the Nuclear Power Institute of China (CNNC) and partners; grid-connected November 2025; reports ~50% increase in net electricity generation and >85% improvement in overall power-generation efficiency versus the prior steam-based system.
notes: Directly fetched; corroborated in Chinese by chinanews.com.cn, cctv.com, and ncsti.gov.cn coverage of the same '超碳一号' project (see L04-053).

title: Malta Inc. – Delivering 300-550C Process Heat (Proman Pampa, Texas deployment)
claim_supported: Malta Inc.'s Advanced Heat Pump (AHP), an ultra-high-temperature heat-pump / pumped-heat-electricity-storage core technology, is deployed at Proman's methanol plant in Pampa, Texas, delivering 300-550C process heat -- described as the first commercial application of ultra-high-temperature heat-pump technology at industrial scale, with Proman's Managing Director quoted on decarbonization and plant-economics goals.
notes: Directly fetched; primary company website with a named customer (Proman) and named customer executive quote, tiered T1 as direct customer documentation. Specific capacity (MW) and installation date not disclosed on this page.

title: Google and Fervo launch first-of-its-kind geothermal project
claim_supported: Google's enhanced-geothermal partnership with Fervo Energy in Nevada, described as "the world's first corporate agreement to develop an enhanced geothermal power project," began delivering carbon-free electricity to the local grid serving Google data centers by 2023-11-28, using horizontal-well EGS drilling techniques and fiber-optic monitoring.
notes: Directly fetched; primary corporate-buyer source. Capacity (MW) not disclosed in this specific post.

title: 全球首台商用超临界二氧化碳发电机组在贵州商运 (World's First Commercial Supercritical CO2 Power Unit Enters Commercial Operation in Guizhou)
claim_supported: State media reports the 'Chaotan One' (超碳一号) sCO2 demonstration at Shougang Shuicheng Steel in Guizhou has entered steady commercial operation (as of 2025-12-20/21); a related 'molten salt storage + supercritical CO2 power generation' demonstration led by CNNC is scheduled to begin construction in Xinjiang in H1 2026, targeting completion by 2028, and has been selected into China's 5th batch of 'first (set of) major technical equipment' (首台套 designation); the article estimates a roughly RMB 100 billion (~$14B) retrofit-market opportunity if all ~300 comparable traditional steel-sintering waste-heat units nationwide were converted to sCO2.
notes: Directly fetched via Chinese-language query. The RMB 100B market-size figure is a single-source vendor/state-media estimate applying only to the steel-sintering retrofit segment; it has not been independently triangulated against a second source this session and should be treated as a rough order-of-magnitude planning number, not a validated TAM.

title: Exclusive: Fourth Power's sci-fi thermal batteries could be cheaper than pricey natural gas power plants
claim_supported: Fourth Power's liquid-tin thermal battery uses TPV cells to convert stored heat back to electricity; the company (co-founded by Asegun Henry, a co-author of the LaPotin 2022 Nature TPV paper) claims cost potential competitive with natural gas peaker plants and far below lithium-ion for long-duration storage. Company has raised funding including a Gates-backed round; a Guinness World Record-holding liquid-tin pump is cited as a key enabling component.
locator: Article body (via WebSearch synthesis; not independently fetched this session)
notes: Vendor cost claims are unverified vendor assertions, not independent validation; tiered T3 pending independent techno-economic confirmation. Not fetched directly.

title: MIT/NREL researchers develop 40%-efficient thermophotovoltaic cell for grid-scale thermal batteries
claim_supported: Trade-press coverage of the LaPotin et al. 2022 Nature paper (L04-001), framing the >40%-efficient TPV cell explicitly as an enabling component for grid-scale thermal-battery products (i.e., the Antora/Fourth Power commercial pathway).
